## Review narrative

Review context: read the canonical file kaola-workflow/issue-725/.cache/review-contexts/f0918ae0c43dfaaa332d2067c7fdd0c28811727a3627e742aae67f4632df140b.json first; confirmed validation_obligations is the EMPTY list, review_phase discovery, epoch 1, attempt_ordinal 1, prior_findings none, plan_hash 4fd7e95ef6792bd9c1ee0af846b49c51c1f358332974e1d349e87b9553ec6f4c. Upstream evidence read before starting: n11-docs (nonce c7dd31ebebb1, echoed above) plus all five writer evidence files (n1-routing-diet fc2d5a8c0215, n3-finalize-adapt-diet 6b82c068ed13, n5-front-agents-diet 2dc6c8beb4a1, n7-role-agents-trim cb98061ee5a1, n9-selfdev-guard c2c63a3d893e) and all five interior gate verdicts (n2 789906e2d675, n4 02c3f39b8ef6, n6 d2cc7441ad15, n8 f029a49dd7d3, n10 5027b7a848d8 — every one domain_outcome approved, verdict pass, findings_blocking 0). All commands below were run from /Users/ylpromax5/Workspace/Kaola-Workflow/.kw/worktrees/issue-725 with real exit codes captured directly (never through a pipe).

RE-EXECUTION LOG — recorded validation command (npm test && node scripts/test-opencode-edition.js && node scripts/test-kimi-edition.js), components run sequentially with $? captured each:
- npm test: exit 1 in 9s. The claude chain died at its 7th step, scripts/test-install-model-rendering.js, with AssertionError "installer rendering should preserve blank markdown lines" (test-install-model-rendering.js:3180). Because npm test chains the four edition chains with &&, the codex, gitlab, and gitea chains never executed in this invocation.
- node scripts/test-opencode-edition.js: exit 1 — "FAIL: H1 (#F3): hookPath resolves a hook via the plugin-sibling ../hooks candidate when the project + config dir have none — got null"; 383 passed, 1 failed. Byte-equivalent to the seeded pre-existing base failure-set.
- node scripts/test-kimi-edition.js: exit 1 — uncaught ENOENT on hooks/kaola-workflow-pre-commit.sh at sync-kimi-edition.js:538 (FATAL before any assertion). Identical to the seeded pre-existing base failure.
The literal && chain is therefore red; the frozen gate claim's "recorded validation command is green" clause FAILS.

FULL FOUR-CHAIN FRONTIER SWEEP — because the && short-circuit hides everything behind the first failure, I re-ran EVERY command of all four chains individually (continue-on-failure, per-command exit + wall time). Result: 59 commands, exactly ONE failure (claude[6]); every other command green, including both re-runs of the long poles:
claude: validate-script-sync 0 (0.1s); test-validate-script-sync 0 (0.1s); validate-vendored-agents 0; bash -n install.sh uninstall.sh 0; package.json JSON.parse 0; test-agent-model-resolver 0 (0.3s); test-install-model-rendering EXIT 1 (7.9s) <- R1; test-install-upgrade-rewrite 0 (2.4s); test-install-manifest-single-source 0 (0.2s); test-validation-runner 0 (0.4s); test-install-adaptive-config 0 (10.0s); test-next-action 0; test-commit-node 0 (8.4s); test-barrier-base-integrity 0 (1.2s); test-issue-probe-memo 0 (0.1s); test-claim-hardening 0 (21.4s); test-adaptive-handoff 0 (0.5s); test-adaptive-node 0 (400.9s, "adaptive-node tests passed (2488 assertions)" — includes the new #736 companion; independently re-run foreground, exit 0 in 401s); test-plan-run 0 (0.1s); test-replan 0 (253.1s); test-bundle-state 0 (1.5s); test-bundle-claim 0 (9.8s); test-bundle-finalize 0 (15.1s); test-sink-merge 0 (17.8s); test-parallel --self-test 0 (0.2s); test-edition-sync 0; test-release-surface-drift 0; test-release 0 (73.3s); test-gap-sweep 0 (0.5s); test-ledger-compare 0 (0.2s); test-validation-allowband 0; test-route-reachability 0 (1.5s); test-agent-profile-parity 0; validate-workflow-contracts 0 (1.6s); test-bash-block-guards 0 (0.8s); test-run-chains 0 (36.8s); simulate-workflow-walkthrough 0 (220.7s, sentinel emitted); test-active-folders-field-parity 0; generate-routing-surfaces --check 0; test-generate-routing-surfaces 0.
codex: validate-script-sync 0; validate-kaola-workflow-contracts 0 (0.3s); simulate-kaola-workflow-walkthrough 0 (17.5s); test-active-folders-field-parity 0; generate-routing-surfaces --check 0.
gitlab: validate-vendored-agents 0; edition-sync --check 0; validate-kaola-workflow-gitlab-contracts 0 (1.5s); simulate-gitlab-workflow-walkthrough 0 (79.8s); simulate-gitlab-codex-workflow-walkthrough 0 (54.4s); test-active-folders-field-parity 0; generate-routing-surfaces --check 0.
gitea: validate-vendored-agents 0; edition-sync --check 0; validate-kaola-workflow-gitea-contracts 0 (1.5s); simulate-gitea-workflow-walkthrough 0 (81.3s); simulate-gitea-codex-workflow-walkthrough 0 (55.9s); test-active-folders-field-parity 0; generate-routing-surfaces --check 0.
Sweep wall time roughly 23 minutes (claude ~18.1 min, codex ~18s, gitlab ~2.3 min, gitea ~2.3 min). So the complete failure frontier over the final tree is exactly three items: R1 (candidate-caused, claude chain) + R2 (pre-existing, kimi) + R3 (pre-existing, opencode). Had R1 not existed, npm test would have been green end-to-end: every other command in all four chains passes on this tree.

AC-D CERTIFICATION WALK (each re-executed by me, not taken from prose):
1. Trim targets met or one-line miss reason recorded: PASS. Independently re-measured with wc -l: finalize command 453 (target 300, miss + reason in n11), adapt command 211 (150, miss + reason), next.skeleton 894 (450, miss + reason), plan-run.skeleton 766 (400, miss + reason), workflow-planner 249 (250, HIT), contractor 208 (150, miss + reason), 11 role agents 1227 total (narration-only, HIT; knowledge-lookup untouched by design). The n11 AC-D table is complete with a one-line reason per miss, plus the recorded reviewer-profiles exclusion (generator-owned; confirmed untouched — empty diff vs base for agents/code-reviewer.md, adversarial-verifier.md, security-reviewer.md, agents/profiles/).
2. Six-surface contracts + route-reachability green: PASS. All five contract validators exit 0 (root validate-workflow-contracts, codex, gitlab, gitea in their chains; the fifth is the byte-twin, covered by cmp below); test-route-reachability exit 0; generate-routing-surfaces --check exit 0 in all four chains.
3. Mirror-before-dispatch on all six plan-run surfaces: PASS. grep shows the <!-- PIN: mirror-before-dispatch --> marker plus the verbatim Band-1 line ("Apply the returned `taskTransitions` to the visible task list BEFORE spawning the role agent — the ledger stays authoritative; the mirror is the operator's only live view", wrapped across two lines) on commands/kaola-workflow-plan-run.md:226, plugins/kaola-workflow/skills/kaola-workflow-plan-run/SKILL.md:378, and all four gitlab/gitea command+SKILL mirrors; the pr-mirror-before-dispatch manifest block is live in templates/routing/required-blocks.js:215 (topic plan-run, both/both, marker + three interior tokens).
4. No provenance token in any prompt surface: PASS. grep -nE "#[0-9]{2,4}\b|D-[0-9]{3}-[0-9]{2}|ADR[ -]?[0-9]" over all 39 trimmed prompt surfaces (6 finalize + 6 adapt + 12 next/plan-run commands+SKILLs incl. forge mirrors + 2 skeletons + workflow-planner + contractor + 11 role agents) returned zero matches (grep exit 1).
5. Byte-twin: PASS. cmp scripts/validate-workflow-contracts.js plugins/kaola-workflow/scripts/validate-workflow-contracts.js exit 0, byte-identical.
6. #736 self-dev guard: PASS. The guard reads the sibling package.json and returns 'claude' when name === 'kaola-workflow', placed BEFORE the opencode tail-pattern in scripts/kaola-workflow-adaptive-node.js (~:815-828) and, per validate-script-sync (exit 0, 26 byte-identical groups), mirrored identically across all four editions; the #736[selfdev-named-kaola-workflow] companion fixture is present in scripts/test-adaptive-node.js:18426 and the suite passed twice (2488 assertions, exit 0 both runs).
7. Recorded validation command green: FAIL — R1 (candidate-caused) + R2/R3 (pre-existing) as above. This is the sole failing AC-D clause.

WRITE-SET ATTRIBUTION SWEEP: git diff --name-only 1491c7e5 (committed + uncommitted working tree) lists 52 paths. Every one maps into the union of the five writers' declared write sets in the frozen plan (n1: skeletons + required-blocks.js + gitlab/gitea validators [the two fossil assertBefore('### Co-active Folders Advisory','## Routing') removals verified as the exact diff] + the 12 regenerated next/plan-run surfaces; n3: the 12 finalize/adapt surfaces + required-blocks.js + test-route-reachability.js + test-opencode-edition.js; n5: agents/workflow-planner.md + agents/contractor.md; n7: 10 role-agent profiles; n9: the four adaptive-node editions + test-adaptive-node.js) plus CHANGELOG.md (n11) and three harness-owned kaola-workflow/issue-725/.cache/*.md evidence files. NO write outside the union. The uncommitted tracked modifications (18 files) are exactly n3/n5/n11 output awaiting the commit boundary, consistent with n11's write-discipline record.

CLASSIFICATION — candidate-caused vs pre-existing: (a) Candidate-caused: R1 only. The Phase D diff removed a pinned heading from the three finalize command surfaces; the out-of-set installer-rendering test still pins it; the claude chain is red and gates the other three chains under npm test. I hunted for further candidate-caused red across all 59 chain commands and both additive suites: none — everything else on the final tree is green. (b) Pre-existing: R2 (kimi FATAL) and R3 (opencode H1/#F3), both rooted in Phase-C-retired hooks/kaola-workflow-pre-commit.sh references in files unchanged vs the run base and outside every Phase D write set, reproduced byte-equivalent to the base failure-sets recorded by n1/n5/n6/n8/n11 and seeded in .cache/run-gaps-manual.md. Both classes block the frozen gate claim (it requires the full validation command green and cannot be waived here); the distinction drives the repair route — R1 reopens n3-finalize-adapt-diet's owner with a fresh baseline (write-set expansion to scripts/test-install-model-rendering.js is a planner decision), R2/R3 route to the planner-owned retired-hook cleanup outside the Phase D node set.

review_attestation: full_review_completed
review_conclusion: The accumulated Phase D candidate is substantively sound — all trim, propagation, provenance, byte-twin, write-set, and #736 guard obligations verify green by re-execution — but the frozen claim fails on the recorded validation command: one candidate-caused regression (the finalize trim removed the '## Validation Delegation Policy' heading that the out-of-set installer-rendering test pins, reddening the claude chain) plus the two seeded pre-existing retired-hook defects in the kimi sync and opencode hooks template; three blocking findings, changes requested.

No interior gate caught it because n4's recorded oracle was the five contract validators + route-reachability + walkthrough; test-install-model-rendering.js runs only in the claude npm chain. Candidate-caused: the assertion passes at the run base and fails only with the Phase D diff applied. Coherent repair is n3's pin-narrowing obligation: either restore the heading on the three finalize command surfaces or re-point the blank-line probe at a surviving '\\n\\n## <heading>\\n\\n' token in test-install-model-rendering.js (out-of-set; needs planner write-set expansion via reopen)."}
